old_claim.note: Political Philosophy (Machiavelli) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Machiavelli's prince-as-supreme-within-territory argument is a direct early-modern conceptual predecessor to Bodin's explicit sovereignty doctrine.

source_shape.source_locator: Early modern political philosophers section: Machiavelli is described as giving the prince supreme authority within his territory, while Bodin is identified as the first European philosopher to treat sovereignty extensively.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: SEP places Machiavelli's territorial-prince argument immediately before Bodin's explicit sovereignty doctrine in the early modern development of sovereignty.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source shows conceptual lineage, not a practical enabling capability, so historical_predecessor is the tighter edge type.

ontology_before: Bodin's sovereignty theory depended on Machiavelli as a generic enabling capability.
ontology_after: Bodin's sovereignty theory keeps Machiavelli as an early-modern conceptual predecessor for territorial supreme authority.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: sovereignty_theory_bodin -> political_philosophy_machiavelli is now historical_predecessor with textbook evidence.
would_reject_if: The Bodin node were narrowed to only the publication event with no intellectual lineage.
A stronger source showed Bodin's sovereignty formulation had no meaningful Machiavellian predecessor relationship.
The Machiavelli node were narrowed away from territorial state power and raison d'etat.
short_reason: SEP frames Machiavelli as a predecessor in the early modern development of sovereignty.
